intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios after an unexpected emergency rate hike.
intake.current_development: An emergency rate hike shocks funding markets, credit spreads, and rate expectations.
known_unknowns: Whether dealers can absorb the repositioning cleanly

summary: Authorities will prioritize market functioning if stress worsens

documents.rate-shock.md: The emergency rate hike triggers repricing across bonds and equities while liquidity stress rises.
documents.funding-stress.md: Funding markets widen immediately and investors debate whether policy credibility has improved or deteriorated.

case_title: Silicon Valley Bank failure and emergency backstop
historical_outcome: U.S. authorities protected depositors and launched emergency liquidity backstops to contain contagion and preserve confidence in the banking system.

sources.title: Joint Statement by the Department of the Treasury, Federal Reserve, and FDIC
The Federal Reserve's Response to the 2023 Banking Turmoil: The Bank Term Funding Program

intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios after the March 2023 Silicon Valley Bank failure and emergency U.S. backstop actions.
intake.current_development: After Silicon Valley Bank failed, U.S. authorities protected depositors and launched the Bank Term Funding Program to backstop liquidity and stabilize confidence.
known_constraints: Financial stability and depositor confidence must be preserved
Authorities also need to preserve policy credibility
known_unknowns: Whether contagion will spread to other regional banks

summary: Authorities will prioritize market functioning and depositor confidence over short-term moral-hazard concerns

documents.joint-statement.md: Treasury, the Federal Reserve, and the FDIC took decisive actions to strengthen confidence, fully protect depositors, and preserve credit access after Silicon Valley Bank failed.
documents.btfp.md: The Federal Reserve established the Bank Term Funding Program to provide liquidity and help banks meet depositor needs during the 2023 banking turmoil.

case_title: Bank of England temporary gilt purchases during the 2022 gilt crisis
historical_outcome: The Bank of England intervened with temporary and targeted gilt purchases and liquidity support to restore orderly market conditions during the gilt market crisis.

sources.title: Asset Purchase Facility: Gilt Sales – Market Notice 29 September 2022
Bank of England statement on end of gilt market operations

intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios during the September 2022 UK gilt market crisis and Bank of England intervention.
intake.current_development: After severe dysfunction in the long-dated gilt market and stress around liability-driven investment funds, the Bank of England launches temporary gilt purchases to restore orderly conditions.
known_constraints: Financial stability and orderly market functioning must be restored quickly
Authorities also need to preserve monetary policy credibility
known_unknowns: Whether the intervention will contain contagion without becoming a permanent backstop

summary: Authorities will prioritize financial stability and market functioning over a rigid commitment to planned balance-sheet sales during the acute stress window

documents.boe-market-notice.md: The Bank of England said it would conduct temporary purchases of long-dated UK government bonds to restore orderly market conditions and postponed planned gilt sales.
documents.boe-end-operations.md: The Bank later said the temporary and targeted gilt purchases had increased market resilience and were intended to give liability-driven investment funds time to address risks.
